---
title: "U-Net Architecture"
---

U-Net is **directly relevant to your work** as a radiologist — it was originally designed for biomedical image segmentation and remains one of the most influential architectures in medical imaging AI.


## Why U-Net Matters for Radiology

U-Net excels at tasks like:
- Organ segmentation (liver, kidney, lung lobes)
- Lesion/tumor delineation
- Vessel segmentation
- Any task where you need **pixel-wise classification**


## The Big Picture

U-Net gets its name from its **U-shaped architecture**. It has two main parts:

```
INPUT                                                    OUTPUT
IMAGE                                                    MASK
  │                                                        ▲
  ▼                                                        │
┌─────────┐                                         ┌─────────┐
│  64 ch  │────────────────────────────────────────▶│  64 ch  │
└────┬────┘           Skip Connection               └────▲────┘
     │ ↓ Pool                                            │ ↑ UpConv
┌─────────┐                                         ┌─────────┐
│ 128 ch  │────────────────────────────────────────▶│ 128 ch  │
└────┬────┘           Skip Connection               └────▲────┘
     │ ↓ Pool                                            │ ↑ UpConv
┌─────────┐                                         ┌─────────┐
│ 256 ch  │────────────────────────────────────────▶│ 256 ch  │
└────┬────┘           Skip Connection               └────▲────┘
     │ ↓ Pool                                            │ ↑ UpConv
┌─────────┐                                         ┌─────────┐
│ 512 ch  │────────────────────────────────────────▶│ 512 ch  │
└────┬────┘           Skip Connection               └────▲────┘
     │ ↓ Pool                                            │ ↑ UpConv
     │              ┌───────────┐                        │
     └─────────────▶│ 1024 ch   │────────────────────────┘
                    │ Bottleneck│
                    └───────────┘

     ◀──── ENCODER ────▶         ◀──── DECODER ────▶
      (Contracting Path)          (Expanding Path)
```


## The Three Key Components


### 1. Encoder (Contracting Path) — "What is in the image?"

The left side **compresses** the image while extracting features:

```
Input: 256×256×1 (grayscale CT slice)
         │
         ▼
    ┌─────────┐
    │ Conv 3×3│──▶ ReLU ──▶ Conv 3×3 ──▶ ReLU
    └─────────┘
         │
         ▼
    MaxPool 2×2  (halves spatial size: 256→128→64→32→16)
         │
         ▼
    [Repeat with more channels: 64→128→256→512→1024]
```

Each level learns **more abstract features** while the spatial resolution shrinks.


### 2. Bottleneck — "Compressed understanding"

At the bottom of the U, you have the most **compressed representation** with the richest semantic information (1024 channels, smallest spatial size).


### 3. Decoder (Expanding Path) — "Where exactly?"

The right side **reconstructs** spatial resolution:

```
    UpConv 2×2  (doubles spatial size: 16→32→64→128→256)
         │
         ▼
    ┌─────────────────────────┐
    │ Concatenate with        │ ◀── Skip Connection brings
    │ encoder features        │     fine-grained details!
    └─────────────────────────┘
         │
         ▼
    Conv 3×3 ──▶ ReLU ──▶ Conv 3×3 ──▶ ReLU
```


## The Secret Sauce: Skip Connections

This is **why U-Net works so well**:

```
ENCODER SIDE                              DECODER SIDE
                                          
High-resolution     ─────────────────▶    Restored with
spatial details     (Skip Connection)     fine details
(edges, boundaries)                       (precise boundaries!)
```

**Problem they solve:** When you compress an image through the encoder, you lose precise spatial information (exactly *where* edges are). Skip connections directly copy high-resolution features from encoder to decoder, allowing precise localization.

**Analogy for radiology:** It's like having both the "gestalt" view (compressed understanding of what's there) and the original high-resolution details to draw precise contours.


## Data Flow Summary

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│   INPUT          ENCODER      BOTTLENECK      DECODER       │
│  (CT/MRI)    →  Compress  →   Deepest    →   Expand    →    │
│                 + Learn       Features       + Combine      │
│                                              Skip Conn      │
│                                                      │      │
│                                                      ▼      │
│                                              SEGMENTATION   │
│                                                 MASK        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```


## Quick Reference Table

| Component | Purpose | Operation |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Encoder Block | Extract features | Conv→ReLU→Conv→ReLU |
| Downsampling | Reduce size, increase receptive field | MaxPool 2×2 |
| Bottleneck | Compressed representation | Deepest conv block |
| Upsampling | Restore spatial resolution | Transposed Conv or Interpolate |
| Skip Connection | Preserve fine details | Concatenate encoder↔decoder |
| Final Layer | Output segmentation | 1×1 Conv to num_classes |
